name: Happy Days Chinese Seafood Restaurant
tagline: One of the last dim sum spots on Oahu with traditional push-cart service — old-school Cantonese in a Kaimuki strip mall.
mustOrder: har gow — shrimp dumplings from the cart
sticky rice in lotus leaf — lo mai gai
egg tarts — warm custard in flaky pastry
parking: Lot at Waialae Ave strip mall
talkStory: One of the few remaining dim sum restaurants on Oahu where carts still roll through the dining room — most places have switched to paper menus. The nostalgic atmosphere and traditional cart service make this a throwback to a disappearing style of Chinese dining. Packed on weekends with multigenerational local Chinese families. The food is solid and honest — not reinventing the wheel, just doing classic Cantonese dim sum the old way. If you miss the dim sum cart experience, this is your spot.
